title: Final AutoGluon full-train refit with bagging on the strongest feature set
summary: Use the strongest existing target-free feature family as the baseline, keep holdout AUC for model selection, then train the submission model on the full training data with AutoGluon bagging and weighted ensembling.
rationale: The current run appears to rely on holdout-style AutoGluon training without full-data refit, bagging, stacking, or weighted ensembling. That can leave useful training rows unused for the final submission model and may increase variance even when the feature set itself is strong.
implementation_hint: Keep the current strongest preprocessing unchanged and report holdout ROC AUC. Then train a second final AutoGluon model on all training rows with num_bag_folds=5, num_bag_sets=1 or 2, fit_weighted_ensemble=True, and auto_stack=False initially. Use that final model for test submission. Increase time_limit to approximately 1800-3600 seconds.
expected_effect: Medium to large potential improvement in public ROC AUC through lower variance and use of the full training set. Confidence is high, but the experiment likely needs a longer training budget.
risk: The reported holdout CV and the final submission model will come from different fitted models, so both must be logged clearly. Bagging may not help if the validation split is unrepresentative, and runtime may increase substantially.
